name: Arweave API Vocabulary
description: Key terms, concepts, and data primitives used in the Arweave HTTP API and protocol.

terms.definition: The native token of the Arweave network. Used to pay for permanent data storage. Subdivided into winstons (1 AR = 10^12 winstons).
The smallest denomination of AR. 1 AR = 1,000,000,000,000 winstons (10^12). All API amounts are expressed in winstons as numeric strings to avoid floating-point precision issues.
The fundamental unit of interaction with Arweave. A transaction can store data on the weave, transfer AR tokens, or both. Each transaction is cryptographically signed and permanently recorded.
A Base64URL-encoded SHA-256 hash of the transaction signature. Uniquely identifies a transaction on the network.
A URL-safe variant of Base64 encoding that uses '-' instead of '+' and '_' instead of '/'. Used throughout the Arweave API for identifiers, keys, and binary data. Case-sensitive.
The collective storage medium of all Arweave nodes — the permanent, decentralized data store that makes up the Arweave network. Once written, data cannot be deleted from the weave.
A cryptographic key pair (RSA or ECDSA) used to sign transactions and hold AR tokens. A wallet address is the Base64URL-encoded SHA-256 hash of the RSA public key modulus.

The public identifier for an Arweave account. Derived as the Base64URL-encoded SHA-256 hash of the RSA public key modulus. Case-sensitive — sending to a different-case address will result in lost tokens.
A collection of transactions bundled together and added to the Arweave weave by a miner. Each block references the previous block forming the chain.
The sequential block number, starting from 0 (genesis block). Used to uniquely identify blocks by position in the chain.
The independent hash of a block — a hash computed over all block fields. Used to uniquely identify blocks and verify integrity.

The ID of the most recent outgoing transaction from a wallet, used in new transactions to prevent replay attacks. Obtain via GET /wallet/{address}/last_tx. Use an empty string for the first transaction from a wallet.
The RSA public key modulus of the transaction signer (Base64URL encoded). Empty string for ECDSA accounts.
The transaction fee in winstons. Must be at or above the minimum returned by GET /price/{bytes}/{target}. Paid to miners and the endowment pool.
The Merkle root of the chunked data payload in a format=2 transaction. Allows verification of individual chunks without downloading all data.
A fragment of a larger data payload uploaded separately via POST /chunk. Maximum chunk size is 256 KiB. Chunks are identified and verified using Merkle proofs.
A Base64URL encoded Merkle inclusion proof that verifies a specific chunk belongs to a transaction's data_root. Maximum size is 256 KiB.
Metadata name-value pairs attached to a transaction. Both names and values are Base64URL encoded. Maximum combined size is 2048 bytes. Tags are indexed and queryable via the GraphQL API.
A transaction tag with name 'Content-Type' that specifies the MIME type of the transaction's data payload. Determines the HTTP Content-Type header when data is retrieved.
The permanent, decentralized web built on top of Arweave. Websites and applications stored on Arweave remain accessible forever through any Arweave gateway.
An HTTP server that provides convenient access to the Arweave network. arweave.net is the primary public gateway. Gateways expose the standard Arweave HTTP API.
Arweave Name Standard 104 — the bundled transaction format enabling high-throughput uploads. Multiple data items are bundled into a single Arweave transaction. Used by the Turbo upload service.
An ANS-104 bundled transaction containing multiple data items packed into a single Arweave layer-1 transaction. Enables millions of uploads per day at lower cost.
The ar.io managed bundling upload service that implements ANS-104. Accepts fiat (credit card) and multi-chain crypto payments. Handles automatic retry and sub-100 KiB free tier uploads.
A transaction status (HTTP 202) indicating the transaction has been received by a node but has not yet been mined into a block.
Each block mined after the block containing a transaction counts as one confirmation. More confirmations mean greater permanence and security.
Arweave's consensus mechanism where miners must prove they can access a random chunk of previously stored data in order to mine a new block. Incentivizes miners to store the complete weave history.
The cryptographic signature scheme used by Arweave for RSA wallet accounts. Signs over a Merkle root of transaction fields using SHA-256.
Transaction format version. Format 1 is legacy; format 2 is recommended for all new transactions, required for ECDSA accounts, and supports chunked data uploads via data_root.
